title: senecio schultzii subsp. chillaloensis
intro: Senecio schultzii subsp. chillaloensis, also known as the East African white egg, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, coughs, and respiratory issues due to its antispasmodic and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Senecio schultzii subsp. chillaloensis include its use as an anti-inflammatory agent, which can help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and asthma. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is sometimes used in traditional medicine in East Africa, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
